title: Vehicle Stability Assist (VSA) System Structure Description - Overview

blocks.text: Vehicle Stability Assist (VSA) System Structure Description - Overview
The VSA system consists of a VSA modulator control unit (with built-in yaw rate sensor and G sensor), wheel sensors, rudder angle sensor, and VSA OFF switch.

The VSA system uses brake control and engine torque control to provide ABS control, EBD control, TCS (traction control system) control, VSA control, brake assist control, creep aid control, hill start assist control, and engine drag control control. The system also includes a city brake active system, depending on the specifications. Some vehicles are equipped with a city brake active system depending on their specifications, and the brake control of these systems is performed by the VSA modulator control unit.
